chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, the topic is backtracking.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is one of the problem solving strategy.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Using this approach we can solve problem or we can write our algorithm by following this

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: strategy.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This strategy uses brute force approach and brute force approach says that for any given

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: problem you should try out all possible solutions and pick up desired solutions.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This we were already following in dynamic programming but in dynamic programming we

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: were solving optimization problems.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Backtracking is not for optimization problem.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Backtracking is used when you have multiple solutions and you want all those solutions.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for explaining backtracking and how it follows brute force approach I will take a

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: simple example and explain the strategy.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us take an example.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If there are three students, two boys and a girl and there are three chairs.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We have to arrange them in those three chairs.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In how many ways we can arrange?

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: There are three number of students are three so we can arrange them in three factorial

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that is six ways.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we want to find out all possible arrangements.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for finding all possible arrangements we can solve the problem and the solution can

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: be represented in the form of a tree that is solution tree and we also call it as state

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: space tree.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will represent the solution in the form of state space tree.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us generate a tree and solve this problem.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us start this is the starting of a solution.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us take all possibilities so first who can sit?

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First chair boy1 will sit so this ad is showing first boy so in the first level we will consider

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: each one of the students so let us say boy1 is sitting there.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now next level for next chair that is next seat who can sit there?

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Boy2 so boy2 can sit here.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The next is girl1 the third level for third seat so girl1 can sit.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we have finished with the input.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now for another arrangement take out girl from here so take out go back here any other

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: possibility no take out boy2 also so take out boy2 and in the second place who can sit

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: there?

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Girl can sit there so girl1 can sit there then at that place next boy2 can sit there.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this one more solution we got.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Take out boy2 take out girl1 any other possibility no so take out boy1 also.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now next is boy2 can sit in first place then who can sit here?

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Boy1 then girl1 then take out girl1 take out boy1 girl1 and boy1 then next girl1 sitting

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: here then boy1 boy2 and here boy1 boy2 and boy1.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So these are the possible arrangements so 1 2 3 4 5 6 so from the root if I follow one

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of the path I get one of the solution.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I wanted all six possibilities and I have all possibilities.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I take this root this says that first chair boy2 will sit then boy1 then girl1.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I have found out all possible solution.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The problems that we solve using backtracking those problem usually will have some constraints

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and we will try to check those constraints and get the solutions which are satisfying

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: those constraints.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now I will impose one constraint here I will say that girl should not sit in middle.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Girl should not come in between then what are the possible solutions?

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us check so again I will just trace and draw the tree.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So first boy1 then boy2 that is near to boy1 then that is at second place then third place

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: girl1 yes correct answer.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Go back next possibility second place girl no not allowed so no need to go further stop

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: here only.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Constraint is imposed and we are not generating the tree further from here and we are killing

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this node we are not going further because what is the condition girl should not be in

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the middle so in the second level girl should not come so we got girl here in this combination

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: so we stop here and we say this node is killed and how this node is killed we say we are

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: imposing a condition and that we call it as bounding function.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That condition is called as bounding function so we applied bounding function and killed

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the node.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is how we are trying to get a proper solution as well as we are trying to save

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: our work also.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now go back boy2 is sitting here boy1 ok second level second chair boy is there no problem

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then girl yes this is also solution.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then go back girl is again coming in between that is second chair so kill this one kill

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the node by applying bounding function.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Go back girl now boy then boy no problem here no problem here then this is also a solution

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is also solution no bounding function if no bounding function is applied until you

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: reach at the last level then you got a solution so total four solutions we got.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: There are few problems that comes under backtracking like n queens problem and some of the subsets

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: problem graph coloring problem I will show those problems.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: One more thing the same approach that is brute force approach is used by one more strategy

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: called as branch and bound.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let me show you the difference between backtracking and branch and bound now itself.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See both follows brute force approach and both generates state space tree but the approach

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is different what is that in backtracking the way we were generating the nodes were

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: first this one then this one then this one then this one go back and come to this side

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: go back so this is DFS we were following.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So backtracking follows def first search whereas branch and bound will follow breadth first

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: search.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will show in branch and bound how we generate the tree first this one then boy1 boy2 girl1

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: see we generate all the nodes in the level this is breadth first search so we are exploring

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: a problem to find a solution breadth wise right so then here who can sit boy1 already

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: has taken the seat so boy2 or girl1 can take the seat so this is boy1 or girl1 can take

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: a seat this is boy1 and boy2 and here girl1 will take a seat boy2 will take a seat girl1

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and boy1 boy2 boy1 so in branch and bound it is generated level wise.

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So only the difference how the tree is generated I have shown I did not discuss branch and

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: bound this we will discuss in some other video it's a different thing but the common thing

chunks.title: Introduction to Backtracking - Brute Force Approach - Abdul Bari

text: Hi, the topic is backtracking. This is one of the problem solving strategy. Using this approach we can solve problem or we can write our algorithm by following this strategy. This strategy uses brute force approach and brute force approach says that for any given problem you should try out all possible solutions and pick up desired solutions. This we were already following in dynamic programming but in dynamic programming we were solving optimization problems. Backtracking is not for optimization problem. Backtracking is used when you have multiple solutions and you want all those solutions. So for explaining backtracking and how it follows brute force approach I will take a simple example and explain the strategy. So let us take an example. If there are three students, two boys and a girl and there are three chairs. We have to arrange them in those three chairs. In how many ways we can arrange? There are three number of students are three so we can arrange them in three factorial that is six ways. Now we want to find out all possible arrangements. So for finding all possible arrangements we can solve the problem and the solution can be represented in the form of a tree that is solution tree and we also call it as state space tree. So we will represent the solution in the form of state space tree. Let us generate a tree and solve this problem. So let us start this is the starting of a solution. Now let us take all possibilities so first who can sit? First chair boy1 will sit so this ad is showing first boy so in the first level we will consider each one of the students so let us say boy1 is sitting there. Now next level for next chair that is next seat who can sit there? Boy2 so boy2 can sit here. The next is girl1 the third level for third seat so girl1 can sit. So we have finished with the input. Now for another arrangement take out girl from here so take out go back here any other possibility no take out boy2 also so take out boy2 and in the second place who can sit there? Girl can sit there so girl1 can sit there then at that place next boy2 can sit there. So this one more solution we got. Take out boy2 take out girl1 any other possibility no so take out boy1 also. Now next is boy2 can sit in first place then who can sit here? Boy1 then girl1 then take out girl1 take out boy1 girl1 and boy1 then next girl1 sitting here then boy1 boy2 and here boy1 boy2 and boy1. So these are the possible arrangements so 1 2 3 4 5 6 so from the root if I follow one of the path I get one of the solution. I wanted all six possibilities and I have all possibilities. If I take this root this says that first chair boy2 will sit then boy1 then girl1. So I have found out all possible solution. The problems that we solve using backtracking those problem usually will have some constraints and we will try to check those constraints and get the solutions which are satisfying those constraints. Now I will impose one constraint here I will say that girl should not sit in middle. Girl should not come in between then what are the possible solutions? So let us check so again I will just trace and draw the tree. So first boy1 then boy2 that is near to boy1 then that is at second place then third place girl1 yes correct answer. Go back next possibility second place girl no not allowed so no need to go further stop here only. Constraint is imposed and we are not generating the tree further from here and we are killing this node we are not going further because what is the condition girl should not be in the middle so in the second level girl should not come so we got girl here in this combination so we stop here and we say this node is killed and how this node is killed we say we are imposing a condition and that we call it as bounding function. That condition is called as bounding function so we applied bounding function and killed the node. So this is how we are trying to get a proper solution as well as we are trying to save our work also. Now go back boy2 is sitting here boy1 ok second level second chair boy is there no problem then girl yes this is also solution. Then go back girl is again coming in between that is second chair so kill this one kill the node by applying bounding function. Go back girl now boy then boy no problem here no problem here then this is also a solution this is also solution no bounding function if no bounding function is applied until you reach at the last level then you got a solution so total four solutions we got. There are few problems that comes under backtracking like n queens problem and some of the subsets problem graph coloring problem I will show those problems. One more thing the same approach that is brute force approach is used by one more strategy called as branch and bound. Let me show you the difference between backtracking and branch and bound now itself. See both follows brute force approach and both generates state space tree but the approach is different what is that in backtracking the way we were generating the nodes were first this one then this one then this one then this one go back and come to this side go back so this is DFS we were following. So backtracking follows def first search whereas branch and bound will follow breadth first search. I will show in branch and bound how we generate the tree first this one then boy1 boy2 girl1 see we generate all the nodes in the level this is breadth first search so we are exploring a problem to find a solution breadth wise right so then here who can sit boy1 already has taken the seat so boy2 or girl1 can take the seat so this is boy1 or girl1 can take a seat this is boy1 and boy2 and here girl1 will take a seat boy2 will take a seat girl1 and boy1 boy2 boy1 so in branch and bound it is generated level wise. So only the difference how the tree is generated I have shown I did not discuss branch and bound this we will discuss in some other video it's a different thing but the common thing I have shown you so this follows def first search that follows breadth first search. So in the next videos I will cover the problems related to backtracking.
